channel.title: Comments for page "Envying the Dead"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Envying the Dead"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written but I prefer Ground Control better as my head canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's quite well-written, but the concept doesn't do anything for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made it longer, added a few details, cleaned up some bleh wording.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is more chilling to me than many of the overtly "squicky" articles, and I enjoyed it very much. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose it could be a personal interest that he indulged in. But in any case, I'll think about some revisions. If it can give the same desperate feeling, I might try to cut down on the different god references.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: definitely haha but both somehow end up working, i guess thats the best part about open ended SCPs like that :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First off, thanks. Secondly, I read your tale, and just…wow. Great story. We really went in very opposite directions, conceptually-wise, huh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. I was really afraid I wouldn't do such a well written SCP justice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Surprised to see a second tale about this SCP that stands quite well on its own. It's one of my favorite SCPs ever, of course, but this is really great, the progression of slowly losing, then occasionally regaining, hope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't THINK of Ellison when I wrote this, but I'm sure there was at least a subconscious influence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of my own 1959 tale Ground Control
, I'm really glad someone else decided to write on this SCP and write it well too, one of my faves

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Indeed. It's a personal favorite of mine, and I just had to upvote after reading how well you described the "Fate Worse than Death" Aleksei suffered much like Ted did in said story.

Whether Ellison's work inspired this or not, I really like, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as I know, materialism and militant antitheism were highly encouraged. So our cosmonaut wasn't likely to be taught much about mythology.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah…No one really knows Nergal do they? I think I might research old Russian pagan traditions or superstitions and maybe substitute some of those for the esoteric references.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not so much a repression of knowledge in this case as what a person of his type would likely know.
Hell, I'd be even OK with greek myth.. it's just that the rest is highly specific knowledge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I don't really know much about Soviet history, focusing more on ancient history myself, but from what I understand, there wasn't TOTAL repression of knowledge. If you were caught, then yeah, it was going to be bad, but they couldn't really stop everyone from learning what they wanted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be honest, the russian patriotism is the bit that bothers me there the least.. he might well be a good communist yet think of himself as a russian first… after all people aren't cardboard models and they do have faults.
The thing with the deities is a bigger problem… I like it very very much , but like Gene says, it doesn't add up under scrutiny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I Have No Mouth and I Must Scream by Harlan Ellison?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, you're right. I don't even know…Bleh. In any case, I feel a bit ashamed. Four years of college history courses! Woo! Well, I guess he's the kind of individual who doesn't agree with the baseline propaganda and continue to think of his home as Russia rather than the USSR? In any case, my bad, I got the dates mixed up. In the future, if I make embarrassing historical mistakes, please point it out. I'd much rather know I screwed up and correct my knowledge than continue in ignorance. Though, in my defense, I always thought the Soviet Union didn't really become…Well, Soviet, until long after the Bolshevik Revolution. Which I got the dates wrong on. Derp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can dream. I can wonder. I can lament.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To me, that's the sign of a good SCP-it makes you think about things not implicitlely brought up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, the USSR was formed in 1922. And cosmonauts usually went to the army after school, became pilots and so on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely not *long* ago - the Great October Socialist Revolution happened in 1917. They.. wouldn't send a grandpa into space. That said, I doubt the czarist regime were fans of mythology. Went to college.. yes, but likely a military one. Soviet cosmonauts were very much not like american ones, they were mostly pilots of the aerial armed forces·

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well he was alive long before there was a soviet union. He'd have to be around…say 30-35 when the "secret missions" happened, and that was very late 50s or very early 60s. As to the mythology, astronauts are smart! He probably went to college. Since we don't actually have any evidence he even existed, I took some liberties giving him a knack for myths. And since the lost cosmonaut theory is just a conspiracy theory, I had no problem saying, "Screw it, they gave him an extra cool version of the suit for this secret mission."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good concept, but some errors stop me from upvoting this tale.

  • SK-1 was used from 1961 to 1963, whereas Leonov, the first man to undertake extra-vehicular activity in 1965, used a Berkut suit. It's debatable, because the suit in question is not exactly an SK-1, but still.
  • Alexei calls his motherland Russia instead of USSR for some reason, and knows mythology all too well for a communist and a military person.
  • Life support in SK-1 is vehicle provided, and even a Berkut suit is only meant to last 45 minutes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay. Since I removed the link, and I don't want anyone to be confused who hasn't read it, this tale is based off of SCP-1959. It's a great one, go read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see your point. You know what, good catch, I think I'll do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You could do it in the comments if you're so inclined.

It kinda just breaks the flow, to be honest. It's not a huge issue, just a little bit annoying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't sure, it just seemed like…I don't want to say the "right" thing to do, because that implies morality in the issue, but it seemed proper to reference back to the main thing. Give credit where credit is due.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only the dead can know peace from this evil
Only the dead can know peace from this evil
Only the dead can know peace from this evil
Only the dead can know peace from this evil
Only the dead can know peace from this evil
Only the dead can know peace from this evil
Only the dead can know peace from this evil

Anyways, I really liked the portrayal of his loneliness, but I really dislike the link on the bottom. The SCP is famous enough to not have a link to it. There's tons of 682 and 173 tales, and none of them link back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the original SCP didn't make much of an impression on me at first, but I read it again, and the implications really sank in. And I thought to myself, "I must write about this."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just don't like the concept— but still wanted to say props for making great use of the criticism of your draft. This is many miles better than the first version I read, you cleaned it up in all the right places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Cosmonaut has always been one of the SCPs that has kept me up. This does nothing but add to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just realized, you and Doktori downvoted Loose Ends. I'm improving! Woo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aight then. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it on the sandbox, like it here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The link at the end is enough. Just use "tale".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really? Crap. What should I put to indicate the right SCP then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Saw this progress in the discussion thread, liked it there, like it better now. +1

You're gonna want to take out that 1959 tag though, that's not an actual tag.
